stance: Risk-on but quality-anchored: lean into cheap, large/mid names where analyst revisions and momentum agree; fund it by exiting dead-signal placeholder lines and richly-valued, falling-revision laggards.
experience_note: This is an early round (last_round_review is null), so I have nothing graded yet and have not re-chased any refuted thesis. I am pre-registering that my edge should show up as: (a) the cheap+rising-revision cyclicals (ONGC, NMDC, NATIONALUM, HINDALCO, GLAND, GRASIM) outperforming the benchmark over the next 1-3 months, and (b) my exits (Vedanta placeholders + falling-revision laggards) NOT outperforming what I bought. What would change my mind: if the value/commodity cluster I am adding underperforms NIFTY 500 over the next quarter while my low-revision exits rally, that says my revision+value tilt is not transferring to returns here and I should revert toward the benchmark-like core. I am watching whether net_flow data returns next round — if smart-money flow contradicts my revision-led adds, I will downsize them. ARM is sized as a small tilt only; no single signal is allowed to dominate a position.
